title: Biblical Commentary on the Old Testament
author: Carl Friedrich Keil and Franz Delitzsch
tradition_notes: Keil was a conservative Lutheran Old Testament scholar; Delitzsch a Lutheran Hebraist and strong supporter of Jewish evangelism. Their commentary is known for rigorous philological and exegetical scholarship, engagement with the Hebrew text, and defence of the historical reliability of the OT. Delitzsch later developed more irenic views toward critical scholarship. OT-only commentary.
notes: Both authors died before 1928 -- text is public domain in all jurisdictions. HelloAO marks this PDM 1.0. English translation published 1861-1875 by T&T Clark. Coverage: 39 OT books only.
known_coverage_gaps: New Testament (by design -- this is an OT commentary)
